text:
Sunset's just past us and it's still a gorgeous evening out there, clear skies, 82 and dry with that low humidity making it feel easy. If you're heading out tonight, mostly clear and comfortable is the story, with lows settling into the low 60s under a light southeast breeze. No reason to think twice about an evening walk or a late dinner on the patio.

Saturday keeps the good times rolling: sunny and warmer, high near 87, with high pressure firmly in charge. The lake backs that up nicely, south winds 10 to 15 knots and waves holding at 1 to 2 feet, so it'll be a solid boating and paddling day, just watch for a bit more chop by afternoon as that breeze builds. Water's a very swimmable 71 degrees. The models are tight on this one, so I'd trust the forecast as given, no surprises expected. Enjoy it while it lasts, since the forecasters are already watching rain work back into the picture by Sunday night into next week.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-24T21:20:00+00:00): Clear, 82F feels 81F, humidity 32%, wind WSW 5 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  This Afternoon: Sunny, with a high near 82. South wind around 5 mph.
  Tonight: Mostly clear, with a low around 63. Southeast wind around 5 mph.
  Saturday: Sunny, with a high near 87. South wind around 8 mph.
  Saturday Night: Partly cloudy, with a low around 65. South wind around 8 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-24T18:15:00+00:00):
  1. Dry and seasonably warm weather will continue through most of the weekend as high pressure remains over the region. Rain chances return late Sunday into early next week.
  2. Unsettled weather is expected across the region for much of next week.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 214 PM EDT Friday... No significant changes to the forecast.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Tonight: South winds 5 to 15 knots. Clear. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Saturday: South winds 10 to 15 knots. Sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Saturday Night: South winds 5 to 15 knots. Partly cloudy. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Sunday: South winds 5 to 10 knots, becoming light and variable. Mostly sunny. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 71F, level 95.68 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-24: highs [GFS 83, Euro 82, ICON 81] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-25: highs [GFS 88, Euro 85, ICON 85] spread 3F; precip chance [GFS 1%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-26: highs [GFS 85, Euro 86, ICON 86] spread 1F; precip chance [GFS 1%, Euro 4%, ICON 0%]
AIR: AQI 38 Good (OZONE) — Expect generally good air quality across Vermont for Friday, July 24th.
SUN: rise 2026-07-24T05:31-04:00, set 2026-07-24T20:27-04:00, UV max 7.25
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high None, low 59: Mainly clear. Lows overnight in the upper 50s.
  high 87, low 63: Sunny. Highs in the upper 80s and lows in the low 60s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: Highs dip to near 78° Wednesday as cooler air settles in behind the front. Showers and a few thunderstorms remain possible through the day, with activity gradually tapering Wednesday night. Thursday turns mostly sunny and more comfortable with a high near 78°, and Friday looks mostly sunny with highs recovering near 81°. The broader pattern looks drier toward the end of next week as weak high pressure finally pushes the trough offshore.
